sourceList.title: Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - GUILLAUME [I] de Dampierre, son of GUY [I] Seigneur de Dampierre & his wife Helvide de Baudémont (-after 1173).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume Ier de Dampierre於1125年出生在法國德 艾斯特奧布特洛也斯時，他的父親Guy Ier de Dampierre34歲，母親Helvide de Baudemont21歲。他和Ermengarde de Toucy至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1174年在法國哈帕格-亞登奧布達皮享年49歲，埋葬在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德福林-利斯-拉斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Guillaume Ier de Dampierre于1125年出生在法国德 艾斯特奧布特洛也斯时，他的父亲Guy Ier de Dampierre34岁，母亲Helvide de Baudemont21岁。他和Ermengarde de Toucy至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1174年在法国哈帕格-亞登奧布達皮享年49岁，埋葬在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德福林-利斯-拉斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume Ier de Dampierre was born in 1125, in Troyes, Aube, Grand Est, France, his father, Guy Ier de Dampierre, was 34 and his mother, Helvide de Baudemont, was 21. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Ermengarde de Toucy. He died in 1174, in Dampierre, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, France, at the age of 49, and was buried in Flines-lès-Raches, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guillaume Ier de Dampierre est né en 1125, à Troyes, Aube, Grand Est, France, son père, Guy Ier de Dampierre, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Helvide de Baudemont, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Ermengarde de Toucy. Il est décédé en 1174, à Dampierre, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, France, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Flines-lès-Raches, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guillaume Ier de Dampierre 1125 in Troyes, Aube, Großer Osten, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guy Ier de Dampierre 34 und seine Mutter Helvide de Baudemont 21. Er hatte mit Ermengarde de Toucy mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1174 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Dampierre, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich und wurde in Flines-lès-Raches, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume Ier de Dampierre è nato il nel 1125 nel Troyes, Aube, Grand Est, Francia, il suo padre, Guy Ier de Dampierre, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Helvide de Baudemont, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Ermengarde de Toucy. Morì nel 1174, nel Dampierre, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Flines-lès-Raches, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Guillaume Ier de Dampierreが1125年にフランスグランド エストアウベトロイスで生まれた時，父Guy Ier de Dampierreは34歳，母Helvide de Baudemontは21歳であった。彼はErmengarde de Toucyとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1174年にフランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアウベダンパエアで49歳で死去し，フランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードフライネス-ルeス-ラチェスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume Ier de Dampierre(이)가 1125년 프랑스 그랜드 에스트 오브 트로예스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guy Ier de Dampierre(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Helvide de Baudemont(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Ermengarde de Toucy(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1174년 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 오브 댐피르에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 플라이네스-e스-라체스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume Ier de Dampierre nasceu em 1125, em Troyes, Aube, Grand Est, França, seu pai, Guy Ier de Dampierre, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Helvide de Baudemont, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Ermengarde de Toucy. Ele faleceu em 1174, em Dampierre, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, França, com 49 anos, e foi sepultado em Flines-lès-Raches, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume Ier de Dampierre родился в 1125 г. в местности троиесь, ауб, гранд эст, Франция, его отцу по имени Guy Ier de Dampierre было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Helvide de Baudemont, 21. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Ermengarde de Toucy. Он умер в 1174 г. в местности дампьирр, ауб, чампагне-арденн, Франция в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности флаинес-лeс-рэичесь, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guillaume Ier de Dampierre nació en 1125, en Troyes, Aube, Grand Est, Francia, su papá, Guy Ier de Dampierre, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Helvide de Baudemont, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Ermengarde de Toucy. Murió en 1174, en Dampierre, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultado en Flines-lès-Raches, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.
